entities.name: V (Vrijen)
entities.context: Career coach advising Daniel on job search strategy and emotional resilience

entities.name: Daniel
entities.context: Engineer receiving career coaching from V on job search optimization

entities.name: Emotional Cost of Job Search
entities.context: V's belief that modern job search is 'a compromise on your soul' — selling yourself in unnatural ways

entities.name: Playing the Game
entities.context: Framework of treating resumes, LinkedIn tweaks, and salary expectations as hoops to jump through rather than reflections of worth

entities.name: Detached Frame
entities.context: V encourages adopting a jaded, almost detached mindset to navigate job search mechanics without internalizing them

entities.name: Content Publishing
entities.context: Regularly publishing short, thoughtful pieces to improve visibility beyond what a resume provides

entities.name: Line-of-Sight
entities.context: Giving hiring managers more context through content so they can see a candidate's thinking and expertise

entities.name: Reducing Friction
entities.context: V's emphasis on lowering barriers to publishing content rather than pursuing perfection

entities.name: Weekly Cadence
entities.context: Suggested publishing rhythm for content — goal-oriented but flexible

entities.name: LinkedIn
entities.context: Platform where tweaks are framed as game-playing moves rather than identity expressions

entities.name: Resume
entities.context: Traditional job search artifact V frames as a hoop to jump through

relationships.context: V coaches Daniel on job search strategy, emotional resilience, and content visibility
V explicitly states modern job search is a compromise on your soul
V frames job search mechanics as game moves to optimize rather than internalize
V encourages emotional detachment as a coping strategy for job search pain
Detached frame is the emotional stance that enables playing the game effectively
V argues regular short publishing improves visibility and provides hiring context beyond resumes
Publishing creates line-of-sight for hiring managers to see a candidate's thinking
V focuses on lowering barriers to content creation rather than demanding perfection
Weekly cadence is the suggested rhythm for content publishing
Reducing friction makes sustaining a weekly cadence achievable
LinkedIn tweaks are framed as one of the game-playing moves to optimize
Resumes are framed as hoops to jump through in the job search game
